options: Should be screened and treated to prevent pyelonephritis
s1.explain: Asymptomatic bacteriuria (2-10% of pregnancies) should be screened (urine culture at booking) and treated to prevent progression to pyelonephritis.

s1.q: The gold standard for diagnosing UTI in pregnancy is:
options: Urine culture with significant bacteriuria (≥10⁵ CFU/mL)
s1.explain: Urine culture with ≥10⁵ CFU/mL is the gold standard. Urine dipstick may be used for screening but has lower accuracy.

s2.q: Safe antibiotics for UTI in pregnancy include all EXCEPT:
s2.explain: Penicillins, cephalosporins, and nitrofurantoin are safe. Tetracyclines, fluoroquinolones, and sulfonamides near term should be avoided.
